meaning: n.中间派议员,中立派议员
examples: He had left the movement because it had abandoned its centrist policies.
Some time later he heard that the ship Beagle was going on a trip to South America and needed a centrist.
What is more, some say their centrist role is overstated.
Given that feeling, voters were most likely to describe the proposal as centrist (37%).
The White House, eager to put on a centrist smile, adopted 1099 repeal as its own.
